description: This figure consists of two sets of tensile mechanical property curves and three transmission electron microscopy (TEM) micrographs, investigating Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C low-density steels with 0, 3, 6 wt.% Cr (denoted 0Cr, 3Cr, 6Cr) before and after 20% reduction cold rolling. The upper two plots display true stress (solid lines, left y-axis, unit MPa) and strain hardening rate (dσ/dε, dashed lines, right y-axis, unit MPa) against true strain, with the left plot for pre-cold rolling steels and the right for 20% cold-rolled steels. The lower three TEM bright-field images (scale bar 500 nm) with [110] selected area electron diffraction patterns correspond to cold-rolled 0Cr, 3Cr, 6Cr steels respectively, showing {111} slip bands whose spacing decreases with increasing Cr content. Key trends: after cold rolling, ultimate tensile strength and strain hardening rate rise with increasing Cr content, while elongation slightly decreases. This figure supports that higher Cr addition promotes formation of finer slip bands during cold rolling, enhancing strength and strain hardening ability via stronger dislocation hindrance effect.
